page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7a-nps1.16xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7T83 processor. Operating at a clock speed of 2.55 GHz, this instance features 64 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core configuration, resulting in 64 vCPUs. The underlying virtualization is managed via the KVM hypervisor.

The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps. The instance does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a resource allocation perspective, the dedicated physical cores and 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio position this instance for memory-intensive workloads that require predictable CPU execution without hyperthreading overhead. The absence of local storage and GPU hardware represents a clear tradeoff, focusing the hardware profile entirely on memory capacity and dedicated compute resources. This configuration is suitable for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and enterprise workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7a-nps1.16xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance powered by a 2.55 GHz AMD EPYC 7T83 processor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, providing 64 vCPUs under a KVM hypervisor. The instance is configured with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, enterprise databases, and applications requiring dedicated, non-hyperthreaded CPU resources.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance featuring sixty-four non-threaded cores and five hundred twelve gigabytes of memory for memory-intensive workloads.

512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
KVM hypervisor virtualization on x86_64 architecture

Designed for memory-intensive enterprise databases and non-hyperthreaded workloads
